headers.//: NEVER mark js/css immutable here: these filenames are stable, not content-hashed, so immutable freezes a returning visitor on an old build for a year. That shipped once and left everyone with JS predating the theme toggle — the button rendered, the CSS was fresh, and nothing wired the click. must-revalidate costs one cheap 304 per load and cannot go stale.
Images are the bulk of the bytes and rarely change, but they DO get replaced in place — so cache hard, without immutable, to keep a swap from being invisible for a year.
